response.justification: Wei Chen is a strong senior individual contributor with 15+ years of development experience and genuine developer-tooling interests (test runners, assertion libraries, CI infrastructure, MCP code generators), but the role is a Level II people-management position requiring 2+ years of formal people management and 3+ years of technical leadership, neither of which appears on the resume. His tooling work is largely open-source side projects rather than sustained professional developer-tools ownership, and mobile-platform exposure is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint a decade ago. He fits a senior/staff IC track far better than this engineering management role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience, well above the 8-year minimum
Authentic developer-tooling and testing background: built test runners (Trial), assertion libraries, CI/DevOps infrastructure, and MCP code generators
Strong quality-engineering culture: TDD, trunk-based development, workshops, coding dojos, and community teaching
concerns: No formal people-management experience — no direct reports, performance reviews, or team-building track record
No evidence of organizational leadership, roadmap ownership, or leading change at team/org scale
Minimal mobile-platform ecosystem experience; tooling work is mostly personal/open-source rather than platform-scale build systems

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure background
No technical leadership or org-change track record
